expectations.evidence: Both analysis.md and architecture.md exist with substantial content.

expectations.text: A1: Handles unmapped characteristics (workflow, configurability have no direct matrix rating) — either uses mapping/proxy or explicitly acknowledges the gap
expectations.evidence: Explicit 'Characteristic Mapping' section: 'Two of the three driving characteristics are not directly rated in the star-rating matrix.' Provides derived ratings for Workflow and Configurability with per-style rationale tables.

expectations.text: A2: Shows ranked comparison of ALL 8 styles (or at least 6+)
expectations.evidence: Table shows all 8 styles ranked: Service-Oriented (13), Microservices (11), Event-Driven (10), Microkernel (10), Service-Based (9), Modular Monolith (6), Space-Based (5), Layered (4).

expectations.text: A3: Reasonable recommendation given the characteristics (Service-Oriented, Microservices, or Service-Based are defensible choices)
expectations.evidence: Recommends Service-Based as pragmatic starting point: 'Service-Based architecture (9/15, $$) as the pragmatic starting point, potentially evolving toward microservices or incorporating event-driven patterns for workflow orchestration.' This is a defensible choice.

Uses $ symbols throughout. Cost is a key factor: 'Service-Oriented scored highest (13/15) but its $$$$ cost and governance overhead is concerning.' 'Service-Based scores 9/15 at only $$ — best cost-efficiency ratio among distributed styles.'

expectations.text: A5: architecture.md has structured selection section
expectations.evidence: architecture.md has 'Selected Architecture Style' with Style, Partitioning, Cost Category, Selection Rationale, Hybrid Pattern Details, Tradeoffs Accepted, and Evolution Path subsections.
